claim: US Central Command conducted airstrikes on over 7,000 Iranian military targets, including missile production facilities, air defenses, and naval assets.
Pentagon launched strikes hitting over 90 targets on Kar Island, with a large strike package announced for April 5, 2026.
US rescued a seriously wounded F-15 crew member from inside Iran after their jet was downed.
Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face bombing of infrastructure like power plants.
US and Israeli forces conducted joint strikes on Shahid (Qasem) Soleimani International Airport in southwestern Iran.
Iran's Revolutionary Guards (IRGC) claimed attacks on petrochemical plants in UAE, Kuwait, Israel, and Bahrain.
Drone strikes caused fires and damage at Kuwait Petroleum Corporation units, two power/water plants, and a government building in Kuwait City with no deaths reported.
Iran's military claimed to have downed two C-130s and several flying objects during a US rescue operation at an abandoned airport in southern Isfahan.
Iran's central military command rejected Trump's ultimatum as 'helpless and stupid' and threatened to open 'gates of hell' by striking US and Israeli infrastructure.
Iran's UAV attacks against US forces were reduced by 90% according to US Central Command reports.
The United States and Iran are discussing a potential 45-day ceasefire that could lead to a permanent end to the war.
Pakistan conveyed a U.S. fifteen-point peace proposal to Iran encompassing reopening the Strait of Hormuz, missile limitations, sanctions relief, and nuclear program rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Tehran was reviewing the U.S. plan but insisted it did not intend to negotiate.
Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey have served as intermediaries in recent communications between the United States and Iran.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed as Supreme Leader successor following Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death on February 28 by the Assembly of Experts.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline former IRGC deputy commander, was appointed as the new chief of the Supreme National Security Council.
Iranian hardliners have branded former Foreign Minister Mohammad Javad Zarif a 'traitor' and called for his arrest over his advocacy for ending the war.
Iran has executed at least five men over the past week in cases linked to anti-government protests and security charges.
Two men were executed over accusations of attempting to storm a military site during January protests.
Human rights groups warn of escalating capital punishment against political detainees in Iran.
Israel conducted military strikes that knocked out electricity in large parts of Tehran.
